cases.passage: My mother had been living alone in our ancestral home in the village for nearly two years after my father's passing. She was 66 years old then. At first, my younger brother and I would visit her every weekend, but as our work schedules got busier, our visits became less frequent. My mother, however, was a strong and independent woman. She would spend her days tending to the garden, cooking meals, and helping her neighbors. One day, I decided to surprise her by taking her to the city for a change. I picked her up early in the morning and we set off for the city. She was excited to see the hustle and bustle of the city, and we spent the day shopping and eating out. She even tried some street food, which she had never done before. As the sun began to set, we headed back home, both of us tired but happy.

questions.q: What was the age of the narrator's mother at the time of the story?
questions.explanation: The narrator's mother was 66 years old at the time of the story.

questions.q: Why did the narrator and his brother's visits to their mother become less frequent?
opts: (C) Because their work schedules got busier
(D) Because they moved to another city
questions.explanation: The narrator and his brother's visits to their mother became less frequent because their work schedules got busier.

questions.q: What was the narrator's mother's attitude towards living alone after his father's passing?
questions.explanation: The narrator's mother was a strong and independent woman who continued to live alone after his father's passing.

questions.q: Where did the narrator take his mother for a surprise trip?
questions.explanation: The narrator took his mother to the city for a surprise trip.

questions.q: What did the narrator's mother try for the first time on the trip to the city?
questions.explanation: The narrator's mother tried street food for the first time on the trip to the city.

cases.passage: My mother's 66th birthday was a special occasion. She had been living alone in our ancestral home for nearly two years, but I decided to surprise her by taking her out for a celebration. I picked her up early in the morning and we set off for the city. We spent the day shopping, eating out, and meeting some of her friends. She was happy to see me and was enjoying the day. As we were walking through the market, she pointed out a stall selling her favorite snack, dry fruits. I bought some for her and she was thrilled. She even bought some for her friends and neighbors back home. As the sun began to set, we headed back home, both of us tired but happy.

questions.q: How old was the narrator's mother on her birthday?
questions.explanation: The narrator's mother was 66 years old on her birthday.

questions.q: What did the narrator's mother point out to him while walking through the market?
opts: (A) A stall selling her favorite food
(B) A stall selling her favorite snack
(C) A stall selling her favorite clothes
(D) A stall selling her favorite accessories
questions.explanation: The narrator's mother pointed out a stall selling her favorite snack, dry fruits.

questions.q: What did the narrator's mother buy for her friends and neighbors back home?
questions.explanation: The narrator's mother bought some dry fruits for her friends and neighbors back home.

questions.q: What did the narrator and his mother do as the sun began to set?
questions.explanation: The narrator and his mother headed back home as the sun began to set.

questions.q: How was the narrator's mother feeling during the celebration?
questions.explanation: The narrator's mother was happy and enjoying the day during the celebration.
